prompt: Our Postgres database has accumulated 18 months of schema drift between environments. Production has columns that don't exist in the migration files, staging has stale indexes, and foreign keys are inconsistent. I need a comprehensive plan to restore schema integrity.

The codebase has 3 years of accumulated tech debt in the data access layer. We have 5 different ways to query the database, inconsistent error handling, and connections that leak under load. The whole DAL needs a systematic restoration.

Our CI/CD pipeline has degraded over 2 years of incremental changes. Build times went from 5 minutes to 45 minutes, there are 30 unused workflow steps, flaky tests that everyone ignores, and caching that doesn't work. Restore the pipeline to health.

The monitoring and alerting system has alert fatigue — 200 alerts fire daily, most are noise. On-call engineers ignore them all. The original alert design was sound but a year of copy-paste rules has made it useless. Restore meaningful alerting.

Our Docker images have bloated from 200MB to 2.5GB over time because nobody pruned unused dependencies, layers accumulate, and there are debug tools left from incident response. The images need systematic restoration to a healthy state.

The microservice dependency graph has become a tangled mess. Services that should be independent have circular dependencies, shared databases, and tightly coupled event contracts. I need a plan to untangle and restore proper service boundaries.

Our IAM policies in AWS have accumulated over 100 overly permissive rules from years of 'just add a wildcard' fixes. We need a systematic audit and restoration of least-privilege access across all roles.

The test suite has degraded badly — 40% of tests are flaky, 20% are disabled with TODO comments, and coverage dropped from 85% to 45%. Nobody trusts the test results. I need a plan to restore the test suite to a trustworthy state.

Our Kubernetes cluster has accumulated resource sprawl over a year. There are 50 abandoned namespaces, orphaned ConfigMaps, stale CronJobs that run but do nothing, and PVCs holding data nobody uses. Systematically clean and restore the cluster.

The logging infrastructure has degraded to the point where logs are unreliable. Some services log to stdout, some to files, some to Elasticsearch directly. Log levels are inconsistent, timestamps use 4 different formats, and structured fields are different per team. Restore consistent observability.
